models.name: tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal
models.description: This table records every attempt a user makes on learning activities, including completion status, scores, and attempt details. It tracks how many times a user has attempted each activity and whether they passed or failed. This is the table for finding the history of user learning attempts and completion records. Use this table when you need to: 1. Find how many attempts a user has made on specific activities, 2. Track completion status and scores for learning attempts, 3. Generate completion reports and compliance dashboards, 4. Analyze learning effectiveness and user engagement, 5. Calculate completion rates and success metrics, 6. Audit learning history for regulatory compliance, 7. Identify users who need additional support or retraining.

properties.description: The unique, system-generated primary key that unambiguously identifies a single learning attempt.

An identifier that establishes a relational link to the Person table, associating each attempt record with a unique individual. This foreign key identifies the actor (learner, employee) in the learning event, making it possible to aggregate and analyze performance data at the individual level.

An identifier that establishes a relational link to the Activity table, associating each attempt record with a specific learning activity. This foreign key enables the system to track and analyze performance data at the activity level, allowing for the aggregation of data across multiple attempts and activities.
A foreign key to the registration table. This value may be null.

The precise date and time the user launched or initiated the learning activity. This timestamp, stored without time zone information, marks the official start of the attempt and serves as the baseline for measuring its duration.

The official completion date and time for the learning attempt, marking the moment the user finished the activity. This timestamp represents the end of the learning interaction.It is used in conjunction with the start date to calculate the total duration of the learning session if elapsed seconds is null.
Indicates whether a certain condition for waiving is met in the context of the attempt.
The textual status of the attempt, indicating its position in the learning lifecycle (e.g., 'Completed', 'Partial', 'Not Completed')
Indicates whether the attempt was successful or not means passed or failed or null.
Column representing the score achieved in an attempt.

Stores the textual status of the user's attendance or participation (e.g., 'Attended', 'Registered', 'No-show'). Note: Despite its name containing 'fk', this column functions as a descriptive label and is not a foreign key; it does not join to another table.
The total duration of the user's learning attempt for the specific learning Activity, measured in seconds. It quantifies the time from when the user started the activity until they completed it.
Version of the course being launched in the system.
A flag (e.g., 1 or 0) to identify the most recent or currently active attempt for a user on a specific activity, especially when multiple attempts are allowed.
The number of times a course or activity has been launched by a user.

This table records every attempt a user makes on learning activities, including completion status, scores, and attempt details. It tracks how many times a user has attempted each activity and whether they passed or failed. This is the table for finding the history of user learning attempts and completion records.
properties.queryPatterns: ["Filter by empfk to find attempts for specific users", "Filter by activityfk to find attempts for specific activities", "Filter by completion status to track completion rates", "Join with person_sumtotal to get user details", "Join with tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal to get activity details", "Group by empfk to track individual progress", "Group by activityfk to analyze activity completion rates", "Filter by startdt or enddt for time-based analysis", "Calculate attempt counts and success rates"]
properties.useCases: ["Learning History Tracking: Record every attempt a user makes on learning activities", "Completion Reporting: Track completion status and scores for learning attempts", "Compliance Dashboards: Generate completion reports and compliance dashboards", "Learning Effectiveness: Analyze learning effectiveness and user engagement", "Success Metrics: Calculate completion rates and success metrics", "Regulatory Compliance: Audit learning history for regulatory compliance", "Support Identification: Identify users who need additional support or retraining", "Progress Tracking: Monitor individual user learning progress over time"]

properties.description: Links attempts to persons for user performance tracking
Links attempts to activities for learning progress analysis
